role_fit_anchors.5: Direct target function with all central must-haves evidenced.
role_fit_anchors.4: Strong target fit with only a limited, addressable gap.
role_fit_anchors.3: Credible adjacent function; material transferability must be explicit.
role_fit_anchors.2: Remote adjacency or multiple material gaps; not a normal target.
role_fit_anchors.1: Different profession or core qualification mismatch; aspirational only.

career_trajectory_anchors.5: Clearly forward or level-equivalent under the decision policy.
career_trajectory_anchors.3: Trajectory is genuinely ambiguous from supplied evidence.
career_trajectory_anchors.1: Violates a no-backward-progression policy without an explicit exception.

essential_qualification_anchors.5: No essential qualification exists, or every explicit must-have credential is evidenced.
essential_qualification_anchors.3: An explicit must-have credential cannot be confirmed from supplied evidence.
essential_qualification_anchors.1: An explicit must-have degree, license, certification, or admission is not met.

rules: Preferred and nice-to-have qualifications never count as essential qualifications.
The model semantically evaluates every canonical custom policy with an outcome, confidence, and evidence; deterministic code only enforces that structured outcome.
Company quality, compensation, or generic brand growth must not compensate for a dealbreaker.
Apply the same anchors in every independent batch.
